Q25.r: The 2023 CAMPA notification explicitly mandates: 'Employment: Forest dwellers prioritized for plantation work.' The Outcome Budget 2025-26 records employment as a mandatory output indicator: 12,500 person-years generated (plantation + maintenance), with Forest dwellers engaged in plantation + monitoring at ₹3,500 per person annually. CAG confirmed wage payment records. This constitutes a mandatory livelihood pathway (wage employment) explicitly articulated in scheme design documents.
Q25.src: Official Notification No. 6-CAMPA/2009/CGN (2023 update) — Employment clause; Outcome Budget 2025-26 — employment results; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — wage payment records confirmed

observed.calc: CAG-verified wage payment records: 12,500 person-years at ₹3,500/person/year. Treating per-person annual figure as per-participating-household-year (forest dweller households engage one primary worker in the scheme). Observed and independently audited.
observed.src: Outcome Budget 2025-26: 'Local livelihood: Forest dwellers engaged in plantation + monitoring (₹3,500 per person annual)'; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25: 'Employment: 12,500 person-years confirmed (wage payment records)'

Q26.r: Observed ΔY of ₹3,500/person/year is available from CAG-verified wage payment records and Outcome Budget. Observed data governs per framework rule. ΔY = ₹3,500 is 16.7% above the ₹3,000 observed threshold — passes but is near-threshold (within 20%). Note: figure is per-person, not explicitly per-household; if multiple scheme workers exist per household, true HH-level ΔY would be higher.
docs: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — wage payment records (12,500 person-years)
Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25 — employment generation data

Q27.r: One distinct livelihood pathway is explicitly articulated in scheme documents: wage employment in afforestation activities (plantation work + monitoring/protection). Both plantation and monitoring activities are forms of wage labour within the same economic pathway. The scheme notification and Outcome Budget consistently describe this as a single wage-employment pathway for forest dwellers. No enterprise, produce-sale, or market-linked livelihood pathway is mentioned in scheme documents. Score = 1 (minimum pass).
Q27.src: Official Notification No. 6-CAMPA/2009/CGN (2023 update) — 'Employment: Forest dwellers prioritized for plantation work'; Outcome Budget 2025-26 — 'Forest dwellers engaged in plantation + monitoring'

Q28.r: The CAMPA fund is a statutory institutional demand mechanism for plantation labour services. Under the CAMPA Act 2016 and Forest Conservation Act 1980, industries/developers diverting forest land are legally obligated to deposit compensatory afforestation funds into CAMPA. The Forest Department/JFMC then channels these funds as guaranteed wage payments to forest dwellers for plantation and monitoring work. This constitutes an institutional backstop (statutory government procurement of labour services) rather than market-dependent income.
Q28.src: CAMPA Act 2016; Forest Conservation Act 1980; Official Notification No. 6-CAMPA/2009/CGN; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 (wage payment records confirmed)

Q29.r: CAMPA MIS tracks ecological/environmental metrics (sapling survival rate, carbon sequestration, biodiversity indices, water table change) at site/ecosystem level. However, no household-level non-monetary livelihood metrics are tracked: food sufficiency months, food species diversity, fodder availability (livestock feeding days), or medicinal plant access per beneficiary household are not reported in Outcome Budget, Economic Survey, or CAG findings. NEAR-THRESHOLD FLAG: Observed ΔY of ₹3,500/person/year is within 20% of the ₹3,000 threshold. With T3.5 = No, the true economic benefit to forest dweller households is potentially under-measured — non-monetary benefits from the restored forest (fodder, fuel wood, medicinal plants, NTFPs) have real economic value not captured in current MIS.
Q29.src: Outcome Budget 2025-26 (no HH-level non-monetary metrics present); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 (no such metrics audited)

pass_verification.enh_T3.5_flag: FLAG: Observed ΔY of ₹3,500 is within 20% of the ₹3,000 threshold AND T3.5 = No. Non-monetary forest benefits (fodder, fuel wood, medicinal plants, NTFPs from restored forest) are not tracked at household level. True economic benefit likely under-measured. Recommend adding HH-level non-monetary tracking in MIS.

sources_verified: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6
